methods.description: Process for excavating and selecting fossil remains for taphonomic study from the BAT-1 site.

steps.description: BAT-1 remains were dug up following standard techniques commonly used in vertebrate fossil excavations.
steps.searchable_summary: excavate fossil remains using standard vertebrate fossil techniques

steps.description: A total of 6,739 bones were selected for our taphonomic study; this subsample corresponds to all of the large mammal material recovered in the first excavation period.
steps.searchable_summary: select 6,739 large mammal bones for taphonomic study

methods.description: Analysis of skeletal completeness, relative abundance, and bone modifications to understand the deposition and preservation of fossils.

steps.description: Number of Identified Specimens (NISP), Minimum Number of Elements (MNE) and Minimum Number of Individuals (MNI) were calculated following Lyman.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate NISP, MNE, and MNI for fossil samples

steps.description: Skeletal completeness and Relative Abundance of particular skeletal elements per taxa were estimated with the formula by Andrews.
steps.searchable_summary: estimate skeletal completeness and relative abundance using Andrews' formula

steps.description: Kolmogorov-Smirnov tests were performed to compare observed skeletal representation to expected skeletal representation.
steps.searchable_summary: perform Kolmogorov-Smirnov tests on skeletal data

steps.description: The susceptibility of the specimens to transport was investigated by quantifying elements belonging to transport groups defined by Voorhies and Behrensmeyer.
steps.searchable_summary: quantify transport susceptibility using Voorhies and Behrensmeyer groups

steps.description: Rose diagrams and stereographic projections were drawn to determine the possible existence of preferentially oriented remains.
steps.searchable_summary: create rose diagrams and stereographic projections for bone orientation

steps.description: Rayleigh's tests were performed to determine whether bones showed a preferred or a random orientation.
steps.searchable_summary: perform Rayleigh's tests on bone orientation data

steps.description: Bone modification data were recorded after examining the taphonomic subsample under a binocular microscope.
steps.searchable_summary: record bone modification data using a binocular microscope

steps.description: Bone alteration data were analyzed following standard procedures and scales defined by Lyman, Behrensmeyer, and Alcala.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze bone alteration data using standard procedures

steps.description: Bone modification data from BAT-1 LLA and ULA were compared using chi-square tests or Fisher's exact test.
steps.searchable_summary: compare bone modification data using chi-square or Fisher's exact test
inputs: bone modification data from BAT-1 LLA
bone modification data from BAT-1 ULA
